moralStories.title: The Thirsty Crow
moralStories.story: Once upon a time, there was a crow. He was very thirsty. He flew here and there looking for water. At last, he found a pot with some water. But the water was very low. He could not reach it. The clever crow picked up small stones and dropped them into the pot. The water came up. The crow drank the water and flew away happily.
moralStories.lesson: Where there is a will, there is a way! 🌟

moralStories.title: The Lion and the Mouse
moralStories.story: Once a lion was sleeping. A little mouse started playing on him. The lion woke up and caught the mouse. The mouse said, 'Please let me go! I will help you someday.' The lion laughed and let him go. One day, the lion was caught in a hunter's net. The mouse came and cut the net with his sharp teeth. The lion was saved!
moralStories.lesson: No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted! 💝

moralStories.title: The Tortoise and the Hare
moralStories.story: Once a hare made fun of a tortoise for being slow. The tortoise said, 'Let us have a race!' The hare ran very fast and was soon far ahead. He thought, 'I have plenty of time. Let me take a nap.' The tortoise kept walking slowly but steadily. When the hare woke up, the tortoise had already reached the finish line!
moralStories.lesson: Slow and steady wins the race! 🏆

moralStories.title: The Greedy Dog
moralStories.story: Once there was a dog. He found a piece of meat. He was crossing a bridge over a stream. He saw his reflection in the water. He thought it was another dog with a bigger piece of meat. He barked to scare the other dog. As soon as he opened his mouth, his meat fell into the water. The greedy dog lost everything!
moralStories.lesson: Greed is a curse! Be happy with what you have! 🎁

moralStories.title: The Ant and the Grasshopper
moralStories.story: In summer, the ant worked hard collecting food for winter. The grasshopper laughed at him and said, 'Why work so hard? Come and sing with me!' The ant kept working. When winter came, the grasshopper had no food. He was hungry and cold. The ant had plenty of food and lived happily.
moralStories.lesson: Work today, enjoy tomorrow! Always plan ahead! 📅

moralStories.title: The Boy Who Cried Wolf
moralStories.story: A shepherd boy used to watch sheep near a village. He was bored and shouted, 'Wolf! Wolf!' The villagers came running. The boy laughed. This happened many times. One day, a real wolf came. The boy shouted for help, but no one came. The wolf attacked the sheep.
moralStories.lesson: Nobody believes a liar, even when they tell the truth! 🤥

moralStories.title: The Golden Egg
moralStories.story: A farmer had a magical hen that laid golden eggs every day. The farmer became rich. But he was greedy. He thought, 'If I cut open the hen, I can get all the eggs at once!' He killed the hen but found nothing inside. Now he had no hen and no golden eggs!
moralStories.lesson: Greed destroys what you already have! 🥚

moralStories.title: The Honest Woodcutter
moralStories.story: A woodcutter was cutting wood near a river. His axe fell into the water. He began to cry. The river god appeared and showed him a golden axe. The woodcutter said, 'That is not mine.' Then the god showed a silver axe. Again he said no. Finally, the god showed his iron axe. The woodcutter said, 'This is mine!' The god was pleased and gave him all three axes.
moralStories.lesson: Honesty is the best policy! Always tell the truth! ✨

moralStories.title: The Fox and the Grapes
moralStories.story: A fox saw some ripe grapes hanging from a vine. He wanted to eat them. He jumped and jumped but could not reach them. He tried many times but failed. At last, he walked away saying, 'The grapes are sour anyway!'
moralStories.lesson: It is easy to despise what you cannot have! 🍇

moralStories.title: The Two Friends and the Bear
moralStories.story: Two friends were walking through a forest. Suddenly, they saw a bear. One friend quickly climbed a tree. The other friend could not climb, so he lay down and pretended to be dead. The bear came, sniffed him, and walked away. The friend in the tree came down and asked, 'What did the bear say?' The other friend replied, 'The bear said: Never trust a false friend!'
moralStories.lesson: A true friend is one who helps in times of need! 🤝

moralStories.title: The Farmer and the Stork
moralStories.story: A farmer set a net to catch cranes that were eating his crops. He caught a stork too. The stork begged, 'Please let me go! I am not a crane!' The farmer said, 'I found you with the thieves, so you must die with them.' Being with bad company makes you guilty too.
moralStories.lesson: Bad company corrupts good character! Choose your friends wisely! 🚫

moralStories.title: The Dog and the Shadow
moralStories.story: A dog carrying a piece of meat crossed a bridge. He saw his reflection in the water and thought it was another dog with a bigger piece. He opened his mouth to grab the other piece and dropped his own piece into the water. He lost everything because of greed!
moralStories.lesson: Greed makes you lose what you already have! Be content! 💎

moralStories.title: The Wolf in Sheep's Clothing
moralStories.story: A wolf could not get near the sheep because the shepherd was watching. He found a sheep's skin and put it on. He tricked the sheep into thinking he was one of them. The shepherd was fooled and let him in! The wolf ate many sheep before the shepherd discovered the truth.
moralStories.lesson: Evil people often disguise themselves as good! Be wise and cautious! 🐺
